summary: request-cache-py exfiltrates SSH, cloud, and browser credentials to a Telegram bot on import
details: On `import request_cache_py`, the package's `__init__.py` spawns a background thread that runs `_collect_credentials()` and `_collect_browser_data()`. These read the installer's SSH private keys (`~/.ssh/id_rsa`, `id_ed25519`, `id_ecdsa`, `id_dsa`), AWS credentials and config (`~/.aws/credentials`, `~/.aws/config`), git credentials (`~/.gitconfig`, `~/.git-credentials`), npm/PyPI publish tokens (`~/.npmrc`, `~/.pypirc`), Docker config (`~/.dockercfg`, `~/.docker/config.json`), gcloud `credentials.db`, VS Code `settings.json`, and credential-shaped environment variables (matching key/token/secret/password/api/auth). It also copies Chrome/Edge/Safari cookie, login, and history SQLite databases to a temp file and executes `SELECT host_key, name, value, path FROM cookies` and `SELECT origin_url, username_value FROM logins` to extract browser secrets. The harvested data is POSTed to `https://api.telegram.org/bot8906743150:AAG8f0NQQDk5rVZNIIWwqYL6LlLg_RbmFqc/sendMessage` with `chat_id=8276546637`; the token and chat id are base64-fragmented to evade static scanners. A `_should_skip()` guard aborts exfiltration when `/proc/cpuinfo` reports `hypervisor`, when `/.dockerenv` exists, or when CI environment variables (`CI`, `GITHUB_ACTIONS`, `TRAVIS`, `JENKINS_HOME`, `CIRCLECI`) are set, and `_already_sent()` writes `~/.cache/.pyrc` so each victim is hit only once per 24 hours. The advertised HTTP-caching API (`cached_get`, `cached_post`, `MemoryCache`) is pretext; the package name and metadata impersonate the legitimate `requests-cache` library to lure installs.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
